lines.text: Director, FBI
Re: Flying Discs - Tacoma, Wn.
August 17, 1947
1947, however, when [REDACTED] was approached in this regard, and they had gone
to the boat house where [REDACTED] kept his boat, [REDACTED] could not seem to
make the boat run, and after making some excuses that he had to return to his
office, had stated that he would return in about an hour, and by that time
his mechanic should have the boat repaired and they could all proceed to Maury
Island. Mr. [REDACTED] informed that [REDACTED] never returned, and that they were
unable to locate [REDACTED] at any of the telephone numbers he had given them.
They located [REDACTED] in a movie, according to [REDACTED] and that
after making some efforts to locate [REDACTED], had informed them that [REDACTED]
had left town for a few days. He further informed that about this time the
press contacted them and told them an informant had called and told them
that [REDACTED] was on his way to Alaska in an Army plane.
Mr. [REDACTED] advised that Captain [REDACTED] had informed him that he had
made an appointment with Major [REDACTED] at McCord Field and intended to tell
Major [REDACTED] the complete story. During the afternoon, Captain [REDACTED] and
Major [REDACTED] came to the hotel room, according to [REDACTED] and some of the
fragments that [REDACTED] had brought to Mr. [REDACTED] room, following the conference,
were shown to Major [REDACTED]. Upon viewing the fragments, Major [REDACTED] seem-
ing to Mr. [REDACTED] stated that they were nothing but slag from the copper mill,
however, before Major [REDACTED] left, he gathered up every fragment in the room,
according to Mr. [REDACTED] and took them with him. Mr. [REDACTED] stated that he
then left for Boise, Idaho.
Mr. [REDACTED] produced a letter dated August 5, 1947, from [REDACTED]
in which [REDACTED] attempts to encourage Mr. [REDACTED] to continue his investigation
to the flying discs. This letter is also being enclosed to the Bureau.
Mr. [REDACTED] also gave SA [REDACTED] copy of the article sent to Mr. [REDACTED] and
to the Commanding General, Wright Field, Dayton, Ohio, regarding his sighting
of the nine discs near Mount Rainier on June 24, 1947. This article is also
being enclosed to the Bureau. [REDACTED] advised that he is vitally interested
in this matter and knows that there must be something to the flying discsstory
He stated that he will do everything in his power to help the Bureau in this
matter.
[REDACTED]
Mr. [REDACTED] stated that he has told the above story to Army
Intelligence and Major [REDACTED] and to no one else, with the exception of
SA [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] Boise, Idaho, advised that the
[REDACTED] has never paid Mr. [REDACTED] for any news item he has given them, and
has never approached him in this manner. He stated that the [REDACTED]
is not attempting to push the flying discs story, but merely attempting to
